title: Invalid Character Validation with Special Cases

statement.markdown: Implement a function that checks if a given string contains only valid characters, i.e., alphanumeric characters, underscores, and hyphens. Note that the function should be able to handle strings containing special characters. However, it should ignore case, and only consider alphanumeric characters, underscores, and hyphens as valid. Special cases include: empty strings, strings containing all valid characters, and strings containing at least one invalid character. If the string contains at least one invalid character, return False.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, and possibly other special characters.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the string contains only valid characters or not.
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle strings of any length.

hints.text: Start by initializing two pointers at the beginning of the string.

editorial.title: Editorial Title Here
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves implementing a two-pointer solution, one for the current character and one for the next character in the string.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to iterate over each character in the string and check if it's valid.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution can be implemented using a two-pointer approach, where we check each character in the string and return False if we encounter an invalid character.

starterCode.python: class Solution: def is_valid_string(self, s: str) -> bool:
